proof_stages.label: Mobius noncorrelation as prime-number randomness
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines the Mobius function, recalls that its noncorrelation with constants, characters, and automorphic-type structured functions encodes the prime number theorem, Dirichlet-type theorems, and L-function information, and frames the talk as asking what simple sequences can correlate with Mobius.

proof_stages.label: Dynamical substitution sequences and the need for rigidity
proof_stages.summary: Sequences of the form f(T^n x) are introduced as substitution or dynamical sequences, Bourgain-type arithmetic ergodic theorems are said to settle almost-everywhere questions, and exceptional points in complicated systems motivate restricting to rigid zero-entropy dynamics.

proof_stages.label: Known rigid cases: rotations and nilsequences
proof_stages.summary: For circle rotations and tori, Vinogradov-Davenport methods give uniform Mobius cancellation and prime equidistribution, and Green-Tao's extension to nilmanifolds proves the corresponding noncorrelation for nilsequences as part of the linear-equations-in-primes program.

proof_stages.label: Ratner-sequence Mobius disjointness conjecture
proof_stages.summary: The nilpotent setting is generalized to finite-volume homogeneous spaces G/Gamma with Ad-unipotent elements, leading to the conjecture that Mobius is orthogonal to every continuous Ratner sequence and to the related Dani-Margulis-Shah folklore conjecture that prime orbits have the same closure and equidistribution as integer orbits.

proof_stages.label: The SL(2,R)/SL(2,Z) horocycle test case
proof_stages.summary: The semisimple test case is specialized to the horocycle flow on SL(2,R)/SL(2,Z), where Dani's trichotomy gives periodic, closed-horocycle, and dense orbits, with the dense case identified as the first genuinely new hard case beyond Dirichlet and Vinogradov.

proof_stages.label: Absolute continuity of prime-orbit limits
proof_stages.summary: For a dense starting point in the horocycle system, the speaker states the proved result that empirical measures supported on prime iterates have no escape of mass and every weak limit is absolutely continuous with bounded density, though not yet known to be invariant or Haar.

proof_stages.label: Conditional lower bounds for long closed horocycles
proof_stages.summary: For starting points that themselves vary with n along period-n closed horocycles, a conditional theorem gives lower bounds and density for prime iterates, assuming optimal p-adic Selberg/Ramanujan expander estimates.

proof_stages.label: Vinogradov type-I/type-II mechanism and effective Ratner inputs
proof_stages.summary: The proof strategy is explained: effective Dani-Ratner equidistribution supplies type-I estimates and almost-prime results, while true prime results require Vinogradov type-II bilinear correlation estimates on product spaces, obtained only conditionally through optimal expansion and shifted-convolution/Ramanujan technology.

examples.name: Doubling map encoding the Mobius function
examples.note: The map x -> 2x on the unit interval, with a point whose dyadic expansion encodes Mobius, is a counterexample showing why arbitrary chaotic systems cannot satisfy an all-points disjointness statement and thus forces the restriction to rigid dynamics.
examples.engagement_reason: The doubling map is explicitly used to build a bad point whose dyadic expansion encodes Mobius, showing how a chosen observable then correlates with Mobius.

passes.reason: The doubling map with a dyadic point chosen to encode Mobius is explicitly used to show an all-starting-points noncorrelation claim can fail.
